# Project Status Report — Move Ledger Plugin to AI Insights

**Plan:** 2026-03-26-move-ledger-plugin-to-ai-insights  
**Date:** 2026-03-26  
**Status:** COMPLETE — 7/7 work packages delivered  
**Generated by:** Head of Operations (Synthesis)

---

## Executive Summary

The ledger plugin has been successfully migrated out of the `@mistralys/persona-builder` npm library and into the `ai-insights-dev` monorepo as a locally-maintained CommonJS module. The plugin now lives at `personas/plugins/ledger/` and is used directly by `personas/persona-build.config.js` via a local `require()` path.

`@mistralys/persona-builder` has been bumped to **v2.0.0** — a major release reflecting the breaking removal of the `./plugins/ledger` sub-path export. The library now ships two entry points (`index`, `cli`) rather than three. All 228 remaining tests pass cleanly.

The full integration test suite (50 ledger plugin tests) has been ported to `scripts/tests/ledger-plugin.test.js` in ai-insights and confirmed passing under Vitest 4.x.

## Strategic Recommendations (Gold Nuggets)

### 1. Add `scripts/tests/README.md` — Medium Priority

Both Developer and QA flagged (independently) that `scripts/tests/` has no documentation explaining the `createRequire(import.meta.url)` bridge pattern required for importing CJS modules from ESM Vitest test files. Any future contributor adding tests to this directory will hit the same non-obvious behaviour. A brief README or header comment in `ledger-plugin.test.js` covering (a) why `.test.js` uses ESM syntax, (b) the `createRequire` pattern, and (c) the absence of `"type": "module"` in `package.json` would prevent confusion.

### 2. Clean up `dist/plugins/ledger/` Empty Directory — Low Priority

`tsup`'s `clean: true` option removes files but not empty parent directories. After the ledger plugin removal, `ai-persona-builder-STABLE/dist/plugins/ledger/` persists as an empty directory in every build. This has zero functional impact (no files, not referenced in any export map), but it is cosmetically untidy. A pre-build `rm -rf dist` step or a `prebuild` npm script would keep `dist/` tidy.

### 3. Resolve Pre-existing Unresolved-Variable WARNs — Low Priority

`node scripts/build-personas.js` emits WARNs for unresolved variables: `{{total}}`, `{{model}}`, `{{cc_name}}`, `{{cc_description}}`, `{{role}}`, `{{number}}` in `wp-decomposer.md` and related templates. These are **pre-existing** YAML source data issues (not regressions from this migration) but they add noise to build output. Tracking them down in the YAML source and either supplying values or removing the variable references would clean up the build signal.

### 4. CJS Module Convention Decision — Low Priority

`personas/plugins/ledger/*.js` uses CommonJS (`module.exports` / `require`) while Vitest 4.x is ESM-native. The `createRequire` bridge works correctly, but this creates an implicit convention that applies to any future plugins added to `personas/plugins/`. Documenting in `AGENTS.md` (or the personas manifest `constraints.md`) that `personas/plugins/` uses CJS and test files use the `createRequire` bridge pattern would prevent future surprises.
